scenario: Hard lockup due to spinlock deadlock with timer discrepancy
symptoms: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 22
Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP

workflow.thought: Check system information to understand environment
workflow.observation: BIOS version and date for hardware context

workflow.thought: Examine tasks in uninterruptible sleep state
workflow.observation: Tasks stuck in I/O wait on CPU 22

workflow.thought: Analyze backtraces of uninterruptible tasks
workflow.action: foreach UN bt | awk '/#[1-4] /{print $3,$5}' | paste - - - - | column -t
workflow.observation: Functions showing I/O wait patterns

workflow.thought: Check timer jiffies discrepancy across all CPUs
workflow.observation: Generate commands to check timer values

workflow.thought: Compare timer jiffies with current jiffies
workflow.observation: Timer base values lagging behind current jiffies

workflow.thought: Get current jiffies value for comparison
workflow.observation: Current jiffies counter value

workflow.thought: Check specific CPU's timer base pointer
workflow.observation: Timer base address for CPU 72

workflow.thought: Examine backtrace of idle CPU
workflow.observation: CPU 72 in idle state with mwait_idle_with_hints

workflow.thought: Inspect the spinlock causing deadlock
workflow.observation: Spinlock state showing waiter and serving tickets

workflow.thought: Check task's stack pointer for thread_info
workflow.observation: Stack address for thread_info examination

workflow.thought: Examine thread_info flags for rescheduling state
workflow.observation: Thread flags showing no reschedule needed

workflow.thought: Check task preemption count
workflow.observation: Preempt count indicating preemptable state

workflow.thought: Disassemble hrtimer_cancel to understand code flow
workflow.observation: Assembly instructions at hrtimer_cancel

workflow.thought: Disassemble hrtimer_try_to_cancel entry
workflow.observation: Function prologue of hrtimer_try_to_cancel

workflow.thought: Read memory to find hrtimer address
workflow.observation: Memory content showing hrtimer pointer

workflow.thought: Calculate spinlock address from hrtimer structure
workflow.observation: Spinlock address derived from hrtimer

workflow.thought: Disassemble around specific address with source lines
workflow.observation: Code flow in hrtimer_try_to_cancel with source context

workflow.thought: Disassemble mwait_idle_with_hints with source
workflow.observation: Idle function implementation with __mwait call

workflow.thought: Analyze exception RIP patterns across all CPUs
workflow.action: bt -a | grep "exception RIP:" | sort | uniq -c | sort -k1nr
workflow.observation: Distribution of exception points across CPUs

workflow.thought: Check watchdog thread affinity masks
workflow.action: foreach 'watchdog/*' task -R cpus_allowed.bits | paste - - - | awk '$8~/watchdog\//' | awk '{c="";for(i=1;i<12;i++)c=c $i" ";print c}'
workflow.observation: CPU affinity for watchdog threads, especially CPUs 64-79

workflow.thought: Verify bit interpretation for CPU 63 affinity
workflow.observation: Bit representation for CPU 63 affinity mask

root_cause_analysis: The exact root cause is unknown at this time.
The hard lockup occurred on the CPU on which the task is looping in the spinlock in kernel mode without giving other tasks a chance to run for more than 60 seconds.
At that time another task had taken that spinlock and was running CPU idle routine.
At that time, almost all CPUs' tvec_base values were way behind the current jiffies value with the only exception of the one CPU on which the hard lockup occurred.
